structured_case.title: Whitaker v. Cole — partnership dissolution and accounting
legal_issues: partner's duty of loyalty and good faith
valuation of partnership assets on winding up
right to formal accounting under partnership statute
relief_sought: Decree dissolving Whitaker & Cole Real Estate Partners effective date of judgment
Formal accounting of all partnership receipts and disbursements from January 1, 2023
Disgorgement of approximately $310,000 in undisclosed management fees taken by defendant
Equitable distribution of remaining partnership assets after winding up
key_facts: Parties formed a 50/50 general partnership in 2018 to acquire and lease commercial properties.
Defendant managed day-to-day operations and bank accounts; plaintiff supplied 65% of initial capital.
Plaintiff discovered in October 2025 that defendant paid himself unbudgeted 'management fees' totaling $310,000 over three years without partner consent.
Partnership agreement requires unanimous consent for partner compensation above an agreed draw schedule.

contract_clauses.name: Partner compensation
contract_clauses.text: Neither Partner shall receive salary, fee, or other compensation from the Partnership except as expressly approved in writing by both Partners.

contract_clauses.name: Books and records
contract_clauses.text: Each Partner shall have full access at all times to the Partnership's books, records, and bank accounts.

cases.name: Henderson v. Brookwood Partners
cases.holding_summary: A managing partner owes the highest duty of loyalty; undisclosed self-dealing requires disgorgement and supports judicial dissolution.

cases.name: Calhoun v. Magnolia Group
cases.holding_summary: A partner is entitled to a formal accounting at any time on showing of necessity; refusal to provide records supports a constructive trust remedy.

cases.name: Thompson v. Greenacre Estates
cases.holding_summary: Specific performance is appropriate for land contracts because each parcel is unique; money damages are presumptively inadequate.

cases.name: Miller v. Pioneer Investments
cases.holding_summary: Fraud in the inducement requires a material misrepresentation, scienter, reasonable reliance, and resulting damages.

favorable_precedents.name: Henderson v. Brookwood Partners
favorable_precedents.holding_summary: A managing partner owes the highest duty of loyalty; undisclosed self-dealing requires disgorgement and supports judicial dissolution.

favorable_precedents.name: Calhoun v. Magnolia Group
favorable_precedents.holding_summary: A partner is entitled to a formal accounting at any time on showing of necessity; refusal to provide records supports a constructive trust remedy.

predicted_outcomes.methodology: Bayesian-style estimate from CAP precedent sample plaintiff outcomes, adjusted for party role and precedent depth.

scenarios.description: Court orders dissolution, full accounting, and disgorgement of unauthorized management fees.
Dissolution plus disgorgement, pre-judgment interest, and capital-contribution adjustments favoring the 65% contributor.
Court allows partial fees as implicitly ratified by long-standing draw practice; reduced disgorgement.

predicted_outcomes.likely_judgment_summary: Court orders dissolution, full accounting, and disgorgement of unauthorized management fees.

win_strategies.title: Force a formal accounting on the statute and contract
actions: Cite the books-and-records clause in pleadings and demand pre-suit production.
Lead with Calhoun v. Magnolia Group on the right to formal accounting.
win_strategies.rationale: The accounting is both the remedy and the evidentiary engine for disgorgement.

win_strategies.title: Frame management fees as undisclosed self-dealing
actions: Cross-reference the compensation clause against bank transfers labeled 'management fees.'
Anchor breach analysis to Henderson v. Brookwood Partners.
win_strategies.rationale: Clear contract text plus fiduciary precedent supports disgorgement and dissolution.

win_strategies.title: Establish capital-contribution equity
actions: Quantify the 65/35 capital imbalance from inception.
Request equitable adjustment in winding-up distributions to reflect contributions.
win_strategies.rationale: Equitable distribution favors a contributor-weighted result when self-dealing is proven.

win_strategies.title: Seek constructive trust over identified proceeds
actions: Trace management-fee transfers into specific assets.
Request a constructive trust under Calhoun's reasoning.
win_strategies.rationale: Constructive trust secures recovery if liquid assets are dissipated.

win_strategies.title: Calibrate dissolution mechanics
actions: Propose a receiver or special master to oversee winding up.
Identify properties suitable for in-kind distribution to streamline closure.
win_strategies.rationale: Practical winding-up plan increases court receptivity and reduces post-judgment friction.

favorable_judgment_tactics.detail: Move for an interim accounting order soon after filing.
Anchor the case to the compensation clause and the labeled bank transfers.
Pair dissolution with disgorgement and constructive trust to maximize recovery.
Use the accounting record to drive a structured buy-out or wind-up agreement.

complaint_outline.content: Plaintiff: Elaine Whitaker; Defendant: Robert Cole
judicial dissolution; breach of fiduciary duty; accounting and constructive trust
Numbered paragraphs tracing formation, capital contributions, the compensation clause, and the unauthorized management fees.
Decree of dissolution; formal accounting from January 1, 2023; disgorgement of $310,000; equitable distribution after winding up

proposed_exhibits.description: Partnership agreement with compensation and books-and-records clauses
Bank statements showing labeled 'management fees' transfers
Key precedent: 210 Ark. 654 (1946) Henderson v. Brookwood Partners
Forensic accounting summary of unauthorized disbursements

pre_filing_checklist: Confirm statute of limitations for breach of fiduciary duty and accounting under Arkansas law.
Issue litigation hold letter for partnership books and bank records.
Verify filing fee and e-filing account for Garland County Chancery Court.
Review formatting requirements (margins, line numbering, word limits).

steps: Open e-filing portal for Chancery Court.
Upload PDF with embedded exhibits; pay filing fee.
Serve defendant per Rule 4 / local equivalent within required period.
how_to_file.note: This system does not e-file on your behalf.

top_precedents.name: Henderson v. Brookwood Partners
top_precedents.headnotes: A managing partner owes the highest duty of loyalty; undisclosed self-dealing requires disgorgement and supports judicial dissolution.
top_precedents.snippet: Partner who diverted partnership opportunities to himself without disclosure was ordered to disgorge profits.

top_precedents.name: Calhoun v. Magnolia Group
top_precedents.headnotes: A partner is entitled to a formal accounting at any time on showing of necessity; refusal to provide records supports a constructive trust remedy.
top_precedents.snippet: The court compelled an accounting and imposed a constructive trust on traceable proceeds.
